verdict_reason: The traditional reading: the karma-kāṇḍa portion of the Vedas takes the three-guṇa-realm as its scope (object/addressees); the imperative is to rise beyond that scope; the qualifications of one who has done so are nirdvandva, nitya-sattva-stha, niryoga-kṣema, ātmavān. The engineering layer maps this carefully to: the operational reflection (a senior who is not captured by team-state oscillation across the three guṇas) while explicitly tagging the analog STRETCHED to mark the scope-difference between the metaphysical injunction and the operational reflection. Reverse-translation: 'a senior who delivers steady work across team-state oscillations between sattva, rajas, tamas — without claiming to have transcended ontology' translates back to nistrai-guṇya as a metaphysical state and the operational analog as a reflection-only-claim. The verse does not invert, weaken, or overclaim. The careful scope-tagging itself prevents distortion. PASS for both 3.6 and 7.10.
